name: Zuccotto (3)
recipeIngredient: 3 tablespoons maraschino or other sweet liqueur
9 ounces sponge cake, cut in thin slices
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 400°F.
2. Toast almonds and hazelnuts on separate baking sheets for 5 minutes.
3. Rub off as much of the hazelnut skins as possible with a rough towel.
4. Chop almonds and hazelnuts roughly and set aside.
5. Mix brandy, amaretto, and liqueur together.
6. Line the inside of a 1.5-liter/2.5-pint pudding basin with cling film.
7. Arrange cake slices in the basin, reserving some for the top.
8. Moisten the cake with most of the liqueur mixture.
9. Melt 2 ounces of chocolate in a small bowl over simmering water; set aside.
10. Cut remaining chocolate into pieces.
11. Whip cream with powdered sugar until stiff.
12. Fold almonds, hazelnuts, and chocolate pieces into whipped cream.
13. Divide cream mixture and spoon one portion into the mold, spreading evenly on cake lining bottom and sides.
14. Fold melted chocolate into remaining cream and spoon into mold to fill cavity.
15. Cover pudding with reserved cake slices and moisten with rest of liqueur.
16. Cover mold with cling film and refrigerate for at least 12 hours.
17. To unmold, cover basin with greaseproof paper and cardboard (or serving plate).
18. Turn basin over onto paper/cardboard; peel off cling film.
19. For decoration, cut greaseproof paper circle 37cm/15 inch diameter.
20. Fold paper into half moon, then triangle, then thinner triangle.
21. Open out and cut out each alternate section without cutting top.
22. Dust the dome with sifted powdered sugar.
23. Mix cocoa powder with sifted powdered sugar; place cut-out circle over dome and sprinkle in cut-out sections.
24. Remove paper carefully without damaging pattern.
25. Transfer zuccotto to flat serving dish using cardboard for support.
26. Serve chilled.
